steps.title: Fastest path: track the angle relationships
steps.content: Track the given angle relationships first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^{\circ}$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are organized.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content:
The angles of any triangle sum to $180^{\circ}$:
$$\angle G + \angle H + \angle I = 180^{\circ}$$
Substituting the given value $\angle G = 112^{\circ}$:
$$112^{\circ} + \angle H + \angle I = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle H + \angle I = 68^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Determine which choice satisfies the constraint
steps.content:
For a valid triangle, both $\angle H$ and $\angle I$ must be positive angles. Since $\angle H + \angle I = 68^{\circ}$ and both must be positive, we need $0^{\circ} \lt \angle H \lt 68^{\circ}$.

Testing each choice:
  • A) $113^{\circ}$ — exceeds $68^{\circ}$, so $\angle I$ would be negative
  • B) $90^{\circ}$ — exceeds $68^{\circ}$, so $\angle I$ would be negative
  • C) $69^{\circ}$ — exceeds $68^{\circ}$, so $\angle I$ would be negative
  • D) $67^{\circ}$ — less than $68^{\circ}$, so $\angle I = 1^{\circ}$ (valid)
That confirms D is correct.
